cooked:
Yes, in the first byte:

00: byte Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)

I had some program test here which loads the images and it work well ;), so you wont have any problem. It uses the mode 1 for decompress.
raw:
Yes, in the first byte:

00:	byte	Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)

I had some program test here which loads the images and it work well ;), so you won't have any problem. It uses the mode 1 for decompress.

raw:
\- alexfont: But then why do the uncompressed saved games load correctly?
I have a theory about that anyway. Maybe the general saved game format is somehow a bit different from the AUTOSAVE.LBA/CURRENT.LBA format. :?

cooked:
Did you check twice what you did for compressed files?? Are you using mode 1??

File formats cant be different from the autosave.

00:	byte	Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)
01:     byte	Scenes Code (see further)	
02:             unused
03:             unused
04:             unused
05:     string  Title
                * = from the end of the title, (after the 0x00)

[b]If the identify flag its 0xA4 the next data is compressed in mode 1
You need to decompressed first after editing it.[/b]

\*00:	s16	Decompressed size
\*02:	        unused
\*03:		unused
\*04...*end:	Data to decompress

[b]After decompress or if the identify flag its 0x24 the next data is an 160x120 Raw Image.[/b]

\*00...*19199	Image data
		 + = after the 19200 image bytes
raw:
Did you check twice what you did for compressed files?? Are you using mode 1??

File formats can't be different from the autosave.



```
00:	byte	Savegame identify, 0xA4 (compressed) or 0x24 (not compressed)
01:     byte	Scenes Code (see further)	
02:             unused
03:             unused
04:             unused
05:     string  Title
                * = from the end of the title, (after the 0x00)

[b]If the identify flag its 0xA4 the next data is compressed in mode 1
You need to decompressed first after editing it.[/b]

\*00:	s16	Decompressed size
\*02:	        unused
\*03:		unused
\*04...*end:	Data to decompress

[b]After decompress or if the identify flag its 0x24 the next data is an 160x120 Raw Image.[/b]

\*00...*19199	Image data
		 + = after the 19200 image bytes
```

cooked:
- alexfont: Yes, I checked. And I also found the difference. After the inventory items, there are 300 bytes in CURRENT.LBA/AUTOSAVE.LBA, but 304 bytes in the general saved games.

Edit: The 4 bytes apparently should be between the images and the inventory.

Edit #2: The inventory part of your format specification is really a bit wrong. Everything after the Protection Spell is wrong.

Edit #3: This is the partial correct sequence for that:

? ? ? Z ? ? ? J C ? ? ? ? ? B D c

Z = Zlitos
J = Jet-Pack
C = Crystal
B = Blowtron
D = Dark Monk's key
c = Clovers

Now Im still looking for where is the Wizards Tunic.

Edit #4: Found out what I was doing wrong when loading the saved games - the decompressed saved games dont have the decompressed size, while I was incorrectly loading them, as if they had, hecen they all began 4 bytes after their real beginning. I fixed this now.
raw:
\- alexfont: Yes, I checked. And I also found the difference. After the inventory items, there are 300 bytes in CURRENT.LBA/AUTOSAVE.LBA, but 304 bytes in the general saved games.

Edit: The 4 bytes apparently should be between the images and the inventory.

Edit #2: The inventory part of your format specification is really a bit wrong. Everything after the Protection Spell is wrong.

Edit #3: This is the partial correct sequence for that:
```
? ? ? Z ? ? ? J C ? ? ? ? ? B D c

Z = Zlitos
J = Jet-Pack
C = Crystal
B = Blowtron
D = Dark Monk's key
c = Clovers
```
Now I'm still looking for where is the Wizard's Tunic.

Edit #4: Found out what I was doing wrong when loading the saved games - the decompressed saved games don't have the decompressed size, while I was incorrectly loading them, as if they had, hecen they all began 4 bytes after their real beginning. I fixed this now. ;)

cooked:
My latest findings about the LBA 2 saved games:
- Part of the car: The translator has to be turned off for the part of the car to appear, but the part of the car doesnt have to be off for the translator to appear;
- Itinerary token: Turn on Pearl of Incandescence, and turn on variable on offset 4EF2 (D + 4B00 + 3E5);
- Super Jet-Pack: Turn on Proto-Pack, and turn on variable on offset 4F38 (D + 4B00 + 42B);
- Blowtron: Turn on Blowgun, and turn on variable on offset 4FA6 (D + 4B00 + 499);
- Offset 4D0E (D + 4B00 + 201): The guards attack you (0x01) or not (0x00);
- Dark Monks key: Turn on Fracos Key Fragment, and turn on variable on offset 4FF6 (D + 4B00 + 4E9);
- Wizards Tunic: Turn on Ancestral Tunic, and turn on variable on offset 4EE8 (D + 4B00 + 3DB);
- Current weapon: 4D7F (specified which inventory object is the currently selected weapon, by inventory object index, counting from 0) (D + 4B00 + 272);
- Horn of Blue Triton life amount: 4F94 (D + 4B00 + 487), max.: 0x64 (100);
Life points: 5109 (D + 4B00 + 5FC), max.: 0xFF (255).

Of course, all of this will be implemented into the LBA 2 Saved Game Editor, which will be released tomorrow.
Woot.
raw: Woot. :cool:
